result.summary: Booking.com exposes Demand API v3.1/v3.2 REST endpoints authenticated with `Authorization: Bearer <API key token>` plus `X-Affiliate-Id`, a Connectivity HTTP API authenticated either by a short-lived bearer JWT minted from machine-account Client ID/Secret or by legacy Basic auth machine-account credentials, and a public no-auth MCP server at `https://demandapi-mcp.booking.com/v1/mcp/8132308`.

demand_api_key.label: Demand API key token
demand_api_key.setup: Sign in to the [Affiliate Partner Centre](https://spadmin.booking.com/partner/login-v3.html) as a Booking.com Managed Affiliate Partner, then generate an API key token for your API user from Partner Centre as described in [Authentication and authorisation](https://developers.booking.com/demand/docs/development-guide/authentication) and [Prerequisites](https://developers.booking.com/demand/docs/getting-started/prerequisites). Copy the token when shown; Booking.com says it is only displayed in full once.

affiliate_id.label: Demand API affiliate ID
affiliate_id.setup: Become a Booking.com Managed Affiliate Partner and sign in to the [Affiliate Partner Centre](https://spadmin.booking.com/partner/login-v3.html). In Partner Centre, locate the `X-Affiliate-Id` for your API user as required by [Prerequisites](https://developers.booking.com/demand/docs/getting-started/prerequisites) and [Authentication and authorisation](https://developers.booking.com/demand/docs/development-guide/authentication). Use that value in the `X-Affiliate-Id` header on every Demand API request.

connectivity_client.label: Connectivity machine account Client ID and Client Secret
connectivity_client.setup: Open the [Connectivity Portal machine account page](https://connect.booking.com/machine-account) and create a machine account using the `API token` authentication type, as described in [Using the token-based authentication scheme](https://developers.booking.com/connectivity/docs/token-based-authentication). Copy and store the `Client ID` and `Client Secret`; you submit them to Booking.com's token exchange endpoint to mint short-lived access tokens.

connectivity_jwt.label: Connectivity API access token
connectivity_jwt.setup: First create a machine account with `API token` authentication in the [Connectivity Portal](https://connect.booking.com/machine-account) to obtain a `Client ID` and `Client Secret`. Then call `POST https://connectivity-authentication.booking.com/token-based-authentication/exchange` with those values, following [Using the token-based authentication scheme](https://developers.booking.com/connectivity/docs/token-based-authentication), to receive a short-lived JWT access token. The docs say the token expires after one hour.

connectivity_basic.label: Connectivity machine account username and password
connectivity_basic.setup: Create or manage a machine account in the [Connectivity Portal](https://connect.booking.com/) as described in [Authentication](https://developers.booking.com/connectivity/docs/authentication) and [Credential-based authentication](https://developers.booking.com/connectivity/docs/basic-authentication). Use the machine account `username` and `password`, Base64-encoded as `username:password`, in the `Authorization: Basic ...` header. Booking.com notes this method is planned to sunset on 31 Dec 2025.
